cards.back: A mathematical function that describes the probability distribution of a discrete random variable, assigning probabilities to distinct values or outcomes.
A mathematical function that describes the probability distribution of a continuous random variable, representing the probability per unit of the variable's range.
A variable whose possible values represent outcomes or events in an experiment or situation, used to model real-world phenomena.
The set of all possible values a random variable can take, also known as its range or domain.
The long-run average value of a discrete random variable, calculated as the sum of each value multiplied by its probability, or the integral of the function over its support for continuous variables.
